_meta.name: Training speed physical ceilings — ALM + CLM
_meta.description: Single source of truth for ALM/CLM training speed measurements, MFU, headroom, and remaining levers. Updated as new runs are measured.
_meta.status: in_progress — #24 r8 ceiling partial (r8a/r8c measured, r8c FAILED), #26 batch/seq sweep DONE, #27 nn_core Tensor port pending, #29 r8c FP8 FAIL-closed

ALM.track: Qwen2.5-14B LoRA fine-tune on H100 (hexa launcher + embedded Python)

hardware.source_note: NVIDIA NeMo published Llama2-70B LoRA benchmarks, r7 measurement this session
hardware.pod_fleet_variance_note: Same software stack varies 31% step/min across pods (xhq9b2c8fljdyo vs ubzp169igb7tns). Runpod H100 SXM pods have heterogeneous performance. MFU measurements should tag pod_id.

r6.notes: Audit miscalibrated — real gain is from fused AdamW + TF32 + cudnn + pin_memory only

caveats: Not a clean Unsloth A/B — transformers 4.45→4.56, peft 0.13→0.18, torch 2.4→2.10+cu128 stack bump blended
Unsloth fast LoRA kernels actually DISABLED (48 layers patched, 0 kernels) due to lora_dropout=0.05
FA2 wheel broken on torch 2.10 → xformers fallback
r7.bonus: Loss descending faster at same step (5.80 @ step270 vs r6 ~10-11), phi_holo 4x at step200

batch_seq_sweep_2026_04_15.pod: xhq9b2c8fljdyo (r6 pod, freed after r6 finished 4000 steps)
key_findings: Micro-batch >> grad_accum — r7 b=1 g=8 was DEAD LAST. Free +8 MFU swapping g_accum→micro-batch.
seq scaling no benefit — seq=1024 wins on tok/s, longer seq loses MFU
VRAM sweet spot ~42 GB (b=8/s=1024), 38 GB headroom preserved
r7's 62.6% is unfair — optimal config same stack would measure ~71% directly

r8c_fp8_2026_04_15.verdict: FAIL — FP8 monkey-patch path broken on HF Qwen2 (random-init loss 15.2 + SLOWER than bf16 at 33.8% MFU)
r8c_fp8_2026_04_15.correctness_fail: te.Linear monkey-patch before from_pretrained causes ALL 48 layers' attn+MLP projections to load with RANDOM weights (HF warning: '_extra_state newly initialized' for every q/k/v/o/up/down/gate_proj). Loss=15 matches uniform prior over vocab 152k, not fine-tuning a pretrained model.
r8c_fp8_2026_04_15.speed_fail: -57.1% vs r7 (51.1→21.9 step/min), -47.2% vs r8a (41.5→21.9). FP8 overhead without compile/cudagraphs + grad ckpt 4x fwd recompute nullifies forward-only FP8 savings for LoRA.
r8c_fp8_2026_04_15.root_cause: Fundamental HF+TE integration gap. Patch disclaimer #1 warned about this. Correct path is accelerate.Accelerator(fp8_recipe_handler=...) OR full NeMo/Megatron native rewrite — neither in P4 scope.
r8c_fp8_2026_04_15.recommendation: ABANDON FP8 retrofit for current HF-based LoRA trainer. r8 ceiling push should pursue batch sweep #26, nn_core Tensor #27, or r8a Unsloth extensions #28.

r8a_stacked_2026_04_16.verdict: MEASURED on SLOW pod, fast-pod equivalent projects ~68-69% MFU — practical 75% ceiling NOT reached, FP8 (r8c) FAILED so r8a remains best r8 datapoint
r8a_stacked_2026_04_16.pod: xhq9b2c8fljdyo (slow, anima-p4-alm-holo) — fast pod ubzp169igb7tns TERMINATED after r7
r8a_stacked_2026_04_16.unsloth_fast_kernels: 96/144 layers patched (48 QKV + 48 O), 48 MLP still disabled (Qwen2 MLP bias term — Unsloth limit)

deltas.vs_unsloth_only_same_pod_clean_AB: +10.1% step/min, +1.6 MFU points (liger + lora_dropout=0 + 96-layer fast kernel unlock)
deltas.vs_r7_pod_variance_adjusted: +18.6% step/min relative (35.0 projected r7 on slow pod → r8a 41.5)
deltas.fast_pod_projection_mfu_pct: ~64-69% (depending on whether to use clean delta +1.6 or relative +18.6%)

next_levers: FP8 via TE — EXHAUSTED (r8c #29 FAILED — HF monkey-patch path broken, 33.8% MFU regression, loss=15 random-init)
Custom CUDA LoRA kernels (Unsloth-style for Qwen2 MLP bias term)
accelerate.Accelerator FP8 path (alternate FP8 integration, different from monkey-patch — NOT yet tried)
r8a_stacked_2026_04_16.agent_status: API 500 hit AFTER state file written, measurements complete and authoritative

stack_blocked: transformer_engine 2.13.0 — wheel 404 for torch 2.10+cu128, source build fails on missing cudnn.h

pod_fleet_variance.finding: SAME software stack measures 33.3 step/min on xhq9b2c8fljdyo vs 48.6 on ubzp169igb7tns at step 50 — 31% slower
pod_fleet_variance.implication: r7's 62.6% MFU partially pod-dependent. Ceiling varies by pod.

r8_partial_2026_04_15.unsloth_fast_lora_disabled_reason: lora_dropout=0.05 — log: 'patched 48 layers with 0 QKV/O/MLP layers'. Setting lora_dropout=0 unlocks +10-20%.
next_actions: r8a: --unsloth --liger + best_batch_from_#26_sweep, 150 steps on freed ubzp169igb7tns pod
r8b: lora_dropout=0 (unlock Unsloth fast LoRA kernels, +10-20% estimated)
r8c: fresh pod with torch==2.9 pin → transformer_engine install → --fp8 measurement (separate track)

remaining_levers.name: compile_unblock (torch.compile via forward_hook)
remaining_levers.status: embedded in train_alm_14b.hexa (commit 021ed178), pending r8 validation

remaining_levers.name: Liger fused kernels (RMSNorm + SwiGLU + RoPE + FusedLinearCrossEntropy)
Batch/seq sweep (38GB VRAM headroom unused)
remaining_levers.expected_uplift: MFU curve characterization, optimal config for r8

CLM.track: Pure hexa from-scratch transformer (CLM d16..d512, byte-level)

mac_cpu_native.note: Published NEON+AMX peak ~200-400 GF/s unreachable without tile intrinsics

h100_gpu_hexa_native.path: hexa-native via cuda_rtc.hexa + cuda_kernels.hexa + gpu_train.hexa (project_clm_hexa_gpu_plan 7 phases done 2026-04-12)

clm_real_step_interpreter.source: bench_train_step_flag_on.hexa (commit 18aaea50), 2-layer minimal decoder, Track B flag ON
clm_real_step_interpreter.config: d16, seq=8, 2-layer, flag ON (Track B cache)
clm_real_step_interpreter.notes: The 27.5x vs historical 27500ms phi-inline baseline is a legitimate speedup in phi compute but tiny vs physical ceiling

clm_phi_block_isolated_interpreter.source: bench_phi_cache_speedup.hexa, Track B cache lookup + anchor + contrastive (commit d8119005)

clm_phi_cache_loader_native_c.source: hexa native compile via 'hexa build phi_cache_loader.hexa -o ...' (commit 28f59cd8)
clm_phi_cache_loader_native_c.note: Best Mac-side MFU so far — but still 71x below 80% practical ceiling

clm_full_loop_native_microbench.honesty: start-dominated — exec overhead per iter, NOT steady-state; real MFU higher once amortized

clm_nn_core_tensor_ported_REAL_STEP.source: bench_train_step_tensor.hexa (commits worktree 36f49f44 + anima merge 15df1e1a + nexus state 640e7e8b)
clm_nn_core_tensor_ported_REAL_STEP.honest_caveat: at this shape (~50K flops/step) FFI-call-bound not FLOP-bound. 1479x is from eliminating list-append O(N²) churn, NOT BLAS throughput. Real 14B shapes (seq=512 d=4096+) expected to unlock additional 10-100x via BLAS
clm_nn_core_tensor_ported_REAL_STEP.train_clm_hexa_integration: deferred — 2170 LOC train_clm.hexa self-test cannot terminate on stage0 interpreter; needs P5a native interpreter rebuild
clm_nn_core_tensor_ported_REAL_STEP.mfu_pct: not yet computed at this shape — FFI-bound regime, MFU unstable

clm_full_loop_native_deployed_POST_HEXA_.source: bench_train_step_native.hexa after #18 hexa_v2.bt74 deployed (commit aabec0a9 nexus)
clm_full_loop_native_deployed_POST_HEXA_.binary_chain: a333004e (original, buggy) → 414b9bcb bt74 (#17 fix, 932960B) → 739fcb6a (with #22 FFI fix, 933032B, live)
clm_full_loop_native_deployed_POST_HEXA_.full_binary_wall_median: 1.08s (module init + phi cache load + 4 train_steps + quantum self-tests, σ=0.013s)
clm_full_loop_native_deployed_POST_HEXA_.runtime_verdict: GO — train_clm_bin runs end-to-end, previous 'array[0]: container is not an array' struct-literal bug RESOLVED

CLM.diagnosis: BEFORE nn_core port: CLM was 3-4 orders of magnitude below physical ceiling on every path. AFTER nn_core Tensor port (#27 1479x) + hexa_v2 full-loop deploy (#18 251x): CLM real-step at 0.681 ms mean on 2-layer tiny shape — FFI-bound regime, MFU not yet meaningfully computable. Next frontier: real 14B CLM shapes (seq=512 d=4096) where BLAS throughput becomes the bottleneck.

remaining_levers.priority: CRITICAL — unlocks the 156.7 GF/s Mac ceiling in real training loop

remaining_levers.name: #17 emit_struct_literal fix deploy (unblocks full train_clm.hexa native binary)
Stage0 OOM fix (PBV list growth, RSS 6.7GB at 250 steps)
remaining_levers.expected_uplift: enables long training runs (currently blocked at 250 steps)
remaining_levers.priority: MEDIUM — unblocks test bed for long measurements

remaining_levers.name: OpenMP parallelism in native codegen
remaining_levers.note: Mostly subsumed by OpenBLAS internal threading

remaining_levers.name: GPU kernel lane for hexa native compile (compose with cuda_rtc.hexa)
remaining_levers.status: partial — CLM hexa GPU already exists per project_clm_hexa_gpu_plan, needs compose with native compile

biggest_wins: ALM pod fleet variance 31% discovered (xhq9b2c8fljdyo slower than ubzp169igb7tns)
CLM Track B cache: 598x phi eval speedup (isolated, commit d8119005)
CLM phi_cache_loader native: 394x vs interpreter (measured, commit 28f59cd8)
CLM Tensor primitive: 4,757x vs list-backed matmul at 128² (isolated, commit 0c29a5c5)
**CLM nn_core Tensor port: 1479x REAL training step (0.681 ms mean)**, commit 15df1e1a
**CLM full-loop native deploy: 251.1x vs interpreter** (1.2228 ms/step), commit aabec0a9
hexa-lang: #17 codegen gap fix + #22 FFI f32→float + #23 parser bracket-depth = 3 compiler improvements deployed
Hexa Tensor primitive + accelerate_blas shim fully wired and productionized
session_summary.alm_status: 62.6% MFU measured on r7 pod; partial 46.5% on slower r6 pod. Ceiling pod-dependent ±31%. FP8 blocked by TE install on torch 2.10. Next: r8a (--liger+optimal batch), r8b (lora_dropout=0), r8c (torch 2.9+TE+fp8 on fresh pod)
session_summary.clm_status: REAL training step 0.681 ms/step (2-layer tiny shape), 1479x vs 1000ms baseline. FFI-call-bound at this shape. Next frontier: real 14B shapes (seq=512 d=4096) unlock BLAS throughput
pending_confirmations: #26 ALM batch/seq sweep (in progress on r6 pod)

update_protocol.rule: Any training run that produces a new MFU measurement MUST update this file before closing the session
update_protocol.owner: Claude Code + training agents (autonomous update allowed)
update_protocol.ceiling_recheck_trigger: Every new major run (r8, r9, ... / CLM native deploy, Tensor port, etc.)
